GREEN HILLS CENTER FOR REHABILITATION AND HEALING

CCN: 445267 · NASHVILLE, TN 37215 · Davidson County

Overview

Address
3939 HILLSBORO CIRCLE, NASHVILLE, TN 37215
Phone
6152972100
Certified beds
150
Avg daily residents
122 (81% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1991-05-09
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.11 4.55 3.48 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.65 0.80 0.55 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.01 1.01 0.86 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.45 2.75 2.08 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.66 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.11 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.57
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.34
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.02
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
68.7%
annual
RN turnover
47.8%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.61
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.17
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-04 10 10 0 44 1 44
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2020-02-07 24 12 12 368 2 552
Total weighted health score
171.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (39)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

32 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
4 G/H/I Actual harm
3 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-02-04 2025-03-05
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-02-04 2025-03-05
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-02-04 2025-03-05
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2025-02-04 2025-03-05
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-02-04 2025-03-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-02-04 2025-03-05
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-02-04 2025-03-05
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-02-04 2025-03-05
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-02-04 2025-03-05
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-02-04 2025-03-05
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. G 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. G 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2024-02-06 2024-03-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2024-02-05 2024-03-05
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2020-02-07 —
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2020-02-07 —
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2020-02-07 —
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2020-02-07 —
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. J 2020-02-07 —
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2020-02-07 —
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2020-02-07 —
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2020-02-07 —
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2020-02-07 —
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2020-02-07 —
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2020-02-07 —
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2020-02-07 —
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2019-02-13 2019-03-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2019-02-13 2019-03-30
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2019-02-13 2019-03-30
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2019-02-13 2019-03-30
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2019-02-13 2019-03-30
